title: 8 Best Lead Enrichment Tools in 2026 (Honest Comparison)
desc: The 8 best lead enrichment tools in 2026 compared: Apollo, Clay, Cognism, ZoomInfo, Lusha and more, with real pricing and who each one is best for.
keywords: best lead enrichment tools, best lead enrichment tools 2026, lead enrichment software, b2b data enrichment tools, best data enrichment tools, apollo vs clay, waterfall enrichment
cta: Want your lead data enriched and your outreach running on autopilot?
body:
Short answer: for most teams the best lead enrichment tool in 2026 is Apollo, because it bundles one of the largest B2B databases, built-in enrichment, and outreach in a single platform starting at $49 per user per month (a free plan is available too). If you need advanced multi-source "waterfall" enrichment, pick Clay. For European data and human-verified mobile numbers, pick Cognism. For the deepest enterprise data and buying intent, ZoomInfo. And if you already live inside HubSpot, Breeze Intelligence (formerly Clearbit) is the native choice. The rest of this guide compares all eight on real 2026 pricing and the honest catch behind each one.

What lead enrichment actually is

Lead enrichment is the process of taking a thin record (often just a name, a company, or a work email) and filling in the missing fields you need to sell: verified email, direct dial or mobile, job title, company size, tech stack, funding, and more. It is not the same as lead scraping. Scraping finds and extracts new contacts; enrichment improves the records you already have. Most outbound teams need both, which is why we also keep a separate guide to the best B2B lead scraping tools. This article is about enrichment: keeping your CRM accurate and your outreach landing.

Why it matters: B2B data decays fast. People change jobs, companies get acquired, and a list that was clean in January is roughly a quarter wrong by year end. Enrichment is what stops your bounce rate from climbing, your reps from dialing dead numbers, and your sender reputation from sinking. Good data is the difference between a campaign that books meetings and one that just burns domains.

The 8 best lead enrichment tools at a glance

ToolBest forPricing starts at (2026)
ApolloBest all-in-one value (data + enrichment + outreach)Free, then $49/user/mo
ClayAdvanced waterfall enrichment and automationFree, then $185/mo
CognismEurope, GDPR, verified mobile numbersCustom (about $15k+/yr)
ZoomInfoEnterprise data depth and intentCustom (about $15k+/yr)
Breeze IntelligenceTeams already running on HubSpotFree with Core Seat, packs from $45/mo
LushaSimple SMB prospecting and Chrome extensionFree, then about $52/user/mo
Seamless.AIReal-time search on a budgetFree, then about $147/mo
BetterContactCheap high-volume email enrichment$15/mo

1. Apollo: best all-in-one value

Best for: startups, agencies, and SMB sales teams that want database, enrichment, and outreach in one tool without stitching five subscriptions together.

Apollo is our top pick for most teams because it does the whole job in one place. It pairs one of the largest B2B contact databases on the market with CRM enrichment, list building, a Chrome extension, email sequencing, a dialer, and a native MCP server so AI agents can pull and enrich data directly. For a lead generation or cold email operation, that consolidation is the real value: you enrich and act in the same system instead of paying for data here and sending there.

Pricing (2026): a genuinely useful free plan, then Basic at $49, Professional at $79, and Organization at $119 per user per month on annual billing (monthly billing runs roughly $59, $99, and $149). Those tiers include about 1,000, 4,000, and 6,000 email credits per month. It is rated 4.7 out of 5 across more than 9,000 reviews.

The catch: credits reset every billing cycle with no rollover, mobile numbers draw from a separate, pricier pool, and real-world spend climbs once you buy overage credits. Phone-number accuracy also trails specialist mobile providers like Cognism. For a deeper look, see our Apollo review and the full Apollo pricing breakdown.

Start with Apollo's free plan here and test the data on your own market before you pay a cent.

2. Clay: best for waterfall enrichment

Best for: RevOps and growth teams that want the highest possible match rates and are willing to learn a more technical tool.

Clay is the power user's choice. Instead of relying on one data source, it runs "waterfall" enrichment: it queries provider after provider in sequence until it finds a match, so your fill rate is far higher than any single database can deliver. Add AI research steps, web scraping, and integrations and Clay becomes a programmable enrichment engine that feeds clean records straight into your CRM or sequencer. If you have ever wondered whether to pick Clay or Apollo, we break that down in Clay vs Apollo.

Pricing (2026): a free plan, then a new structure of Launch at $185 per month and Growth at $495 per month, plus a custom Enterprise tier. In March 2026 Clay split billing into Data Credits (third-party lookups) and Actions (workflow and AI steps); legacy Starter, Explorer, and Pro plans stayed available to existing customers into April 2026.

The catch: there is a real learning curve, and Clay charges Data Credits for every enrichment attempt whether or not it returns a result. Run a wide waterfall on a stale list and you can burn credits fast. It rewards teams that invest the time to build and tune their tables.

3. Cognism: best for Europe and verified mobiles

Best for: teams selling into EMEA who need GDPR-safe data and phone numbers that actually connect.

Cognism's edge is compliance and phone quality. Its Diamond Data set is made up of mobile numbers that have been human-verified, which is a genuine differentiator when your reps live on the phone. It also treats GDPR and global privacy as a first-class concern rather than a checkbox, which matters a lot if you prospect into Europe. Coverage of EMEA contacts is among the strongest in the category.

Pricing (2026): Cognism does not publish prices. Real annual contracts in 2026 typically run from about $15,000 to $60,000 or more depending on seats and credits, with Diamond Data and the higher tiers at the top of that range.

The catch: the sales-led, annual-contract model and the price tag put it out of reach for solopreneurs, and US coverage, while solid, still trails ZoomInfo. This is a tool for funded teams with a real European motion.

4. ZoomInfo: best for enterprise data depth

Best for: large sales organizations that need the deepest US data, intent signals, and org charts, with the budget to match.

ZoomInfo is still the most comprehensive B2B data platform for enterprise. Beyond contacts it layers on buying-intent data, website-visitor identification, scoops, and org charts, and it integrates deeply with every major CRM and sales engagement platform. If data depth and intent are your priority and price is secondary, it is hard to beat.

Pricing (2026): sales-led and annual-only. The Professional tier starts around $15,000 per year (three seats, 5,000 credits), Advanced runs roughly $25,000 to $30,000, and Elite is $40,000 and up. Per-seat add-ons run $1,500 to $2,500 per user per year, and the median contract sits around $31,875 per year.

The catch: it is the most expensive option here, pricing is opaque, and contracts come with renewal increases and a 60 to 90 day cancellation notice. Smaller teams almost always get better value from Apollo.

5. Clearbit / Breeze Intelligence: best for HubSpot users

Best for: teams whose entire go-to-market already runs inside HubSpot.

Clearbit is now Breeze Intelligence, HubSpot's native enrichment layer. If you live in HubSpot, that integration is the whole point: records enrich automatically, forms shorten with progressive profiling, and there is nothing to sync. Following HubSpot's INBOUND 2025 announcement, standard data enrichment is now included free with every HubSpot Core Seat, which covers a lot of teams outright.

Pricing (2026): free standard enrichment with a Core Seat, then paid Breeze Intelligence credit packs starting around $45 per month for 100 credits (annual) on top of your HubSpot plan, scaling to roughly $150 per month for 1,000 credits.

The catch: it really only makes sense if HubSpot is your CRM. The standalone Clearbit product is gone, and your total cost rides on your HubSpot tier, which can get expensive at the Professional level and above.

6. Lusha: best for simple SMB prospecting

Best for: small teams and individual reps who want fast contact lookups without a complex setup.

Lusha is the easy-button option. Its Chrome extension surfaces verified emails and phone numbers while you browse LinkedIn or a company website, and the interface is about as simple as this category gets. For an SMB that just needs accurate contacts on demand, it is quick to adopt and pleasant to use.

Pricing (2026): a free plan with 40 credits per month, then Pro from about $52 to $69 per user per month (around 7,200 credits per year, up to two users) and Premium from about $300 to $400 per user per month (around 40,800 credits per year, up to five users). Scale is custom. A verified email costs one credit; a phone reveal now costs up to ten.

The catch: the database is smaller than Apollo's or ZoomInfo's, pricing is per seat, and phone reveals burn through credits quickly. It is great for light prospecting, less so for high-volume enrichment.

7. Seamless.AI: best for real-time search on a budget

Best for: reps who want a low entry price and a real-time search engine that builds lists as they work.

Seamless.AI markets itself as a real-time search engine for B2B contacts, finding details on the fly rather than serving them from a static database. The entry price is low and the free tier lets you try it, which is exactly what you should do first.

Pricing (2026): a free plan with 50 lifetime credits, then paid plans from about $147 per month, with Pro and custom Enterprise tiers above that.

The catch: be honest with yourself about data quality. Independent testing puts practical email accuracy around 60 to 75 percent and phone accuracy lower, credits are deducted for every lookup attempt including failures, and users widely report aggressive auto-renewals and add-on upsells. Test it hard on your own market during the free tier before signing anything.

8. BetterContact: best for cheap high-volume email enrichment

Best for: teams that already have a list and just want verified emails and mobiles at the lowest cost per contact.

BetterContact is a focused waterfall enrichment service. It cascades through more than 15 data providers, sequences them by geography and industry, and charges only for verified results, pushing email find rates into the 87 to 95 percent range. If enrichment is the only job you need done, it is one of the cheapest reliable ways to do it.

Pricing (2026): plans start at just $15 per month for 200 credits, and you only pay for contacts it actually finds.

The catch: it is enrichment-only. There is no built-in database to prospect from and no sequencer to send from, so you bring your own list and pair it with an outreach tool. That is fine if you already run cold email elsewhere, but it is not an all-in-one.

How to choose the right enrichment tool

Match the tool to your motion rather than chasing the longest feature list:

  • Want one tool for data, enrichment, and outreach: Apollo. It is the best value for the vast majority of SMB and agency teams.
  • Want the highest match rates and you are technical: Clay, or a budget waterfall like BetterContact if you only need emails.
  • Selling into Europe or living on the phone: Cognism for GDPR-safe, human-verified mobiles.
  • Enterprise with intent and budget: ZoomInfo.
  • All-in on HubSpot: Breeze Intelligence.

Whatever you choose, remember that enrichment is step one, not the finish line. Clean data only pays off when it flows into a disciplined send: verified records, warmed inboxes, and tight follow-up. That full pipeline, from enriched list to booked meeting, is exactly what we build and run for clients through our lead generation and cold email marketing services, and we wire the data and sending tools together with AI automation so it runs without a human babysitting it.

One practical tip: before you commit to any paid plan, run a sample of your real target market through the free tier and check the bounce rate yourself. Data quality varies wildly by region and industry, and the only test that matters is your list, not a vendor's marketing number. A free plan like Apollo's is the easiest place to start that test.

The verdict

There is no single best lead enrichment tool, only the best one for your motion and budget. But if we had to put a new team on one platform today, it would be Apollo: it covers data, enrichment, and outreach in one place, starts free, and scales from a solo founder to a full sales org without forcing you to assemble a stack. Layer in Clay or Cognism when you need higher match rates or verified European mobiles, and you have a 2026 data engine that keeps your CRM clean and your campaigns landing.
faqs: What is the best lead enrichment tool in 2026?

For most teams, Apollo offers the best overall value because it combines a large B2B database, built-in enrichment, and outreach in one platform starting at $49 per user per month, with a free plan to test first. Clay is best for advanced waterfall enrichment, Cognism for European and phone-verified data, and ZoomInfo for enterprise data depth.
What is the difference between lead enrichment and lead scraping?

Lead scraping finds and extracts brand-new contacts from sources like LinkedIn or company sites. Lead enrichment takes records you already have and fills in or verifies missing fields such as email, mobile, title, and company data. Most outbound teams use both: scrape to build a list, then enrich to keep it accurate.
How much do lead enrichment tools cost?

Prices range widely in 2026. Budget tools like BetterContact start at $15 per month and Lusha and Apollo offer free plans with paid tiers from about $49 to $69 per user per month. Mid-market tools like Clay run $185 to $495 per month, while enterprise platforms such as ZoomInfo and Cognism are sales-led and typically cost $15,000 or more per year.

Waterfall enrichment queries multiple data providers in sequence, falling through from one to the next until it finds a match. Because no single database is complete, this approach delivers much higher fill rates. Clay and BetterContact are the best-known waterfall tools, though most charge for each lookup attempt regardless of whether it returns data.
Is Apollo good for data enrichment?

Yes. Apollo enriches CRM records, verifies emails, and appends firmographic data, and it bundles that with prospecting and outreach so you can enrich and act in one place. Its main limits are that credits expire each billing cycle and mobile-phone accuracy trails specialist providers like Cognism. For most SMB and agency teams it is the best value enrichment option.
How do I keep enriched lead data from going stale?

B2B data decays roughly 25 to 30 percent a year as people change jobs and companies change. Re-enrich your active lists on a schedule (monthly for hot segments), verify emails before each send, and remove or re-verify any record that bounces. Building that refresh step into your workflow, or having an agency run it for you, is what protects deliverability over time.
